TECHNICIANS COULD ONLY MEASURE THE MIDDLE HOLE OF FIVE HOLES DUE TO DEPTH CONSTRAINTS.  USING THE MIDDLE DEPTH, VELOCITY AND WIDTH OF THE SECTION IT IS ESTIMATED A MAXIMUM FLOW WOULD BE ~0.1554CMS AND A MINIMUM FLOW WOULD BE ~0.0239CMS WITH AN AVERAGE OF 0.0897CMS ROUNDED OFF EQUALS ~0.01CMS.  COMPARISON BETWEEN THE 2016 JAN AND MAR MEASUREMENTS AT KISKATINAW, BEATTON RECORDED DOUBLE THE JAN FLOW IN MARCH AND ALCES RIVER WAS DRY IN JAN AND 0.01CMS IN MAR (~10X HIGHER).  THIS INFORMATION SUPPORTS THE POUCE COUPE ESTIMATE WITH DOUBLE THE FLOW IN MAR FROM JAN. SELECTED TWO MINUTES EITHER SIDE OF THE PANEL TIME COLLECTED AT 15m ON THE TAGLINE TO USE PANEL TIME AS MEAN MQ TIME. Sources:Manual
